title: Lachemilla paludicola
taxonomy: Lachemilla paludicola is classified within the family Rosaceae, commonly known as the rose family. This family is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. The genus Lachemilla is a small group of flowering plants that are native to South America. Lachemilla paludicola is a species of flowering plant that is closely related to other species within the genus. Its classification is further grouped into the tribe Amygdaleae, which includes several other genera of flowering plants.

intro: Lachemilla paludicola, also known as marsh round-leaved Lachemilla, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Lachemilla paludicola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Lachemilla paludicola is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures to support digestive health and boost the immune system.
